changes.result: CONFIRMED — SOURCE CONTENT READ — CRITICAL ARCHITECTURAL FINDING
changes.finding: CONFIRMED. _build_biomarker_rule() at rules.py:131-145 uses direct dict key access: bands['optimal'], bands['normal'], bands['borderline'], bands['high'], bands['very_high'], bands['critical']. No .get() fallback. No default. Missing any band sub-key raises KeyError at rule-load time. All six band sub-keys are mandatory to construct a BiomarkerRule. This is the direct code evidence that the current architecture cannot support a bandless thyroid scoring entry.

CONFIRMED. _load_biomarker_rules() at rules.py:153-156 iterates systems[system].biomarkers and looks up each in the YAML biomarkers dict. Guard at line 155: `if isinstance(policy_item, dict)` — silently skips biomarkers with no YAML entry. Consequence: adding TSH to hormonal biomarkers list without a bands YAML block would result in TSH being silently dropped at load time, never reaching calculate_biomarker_score via system orchestration.

CONFIRMED. calculate_biomarker_score() at rules.py:361-436 CAN score a biomarker using only lab range (input_reference_range param) without a BiomarkerRule. If BiomarkerRule is None (line 395-402), policy_unit becomes empty string and unit coherence check is skipped. However, this path is NOT reachable via system orchestration for biomarkers absent from the SSOT biomarkers block — system orchestration at engine.py:192 iterates only biomarkers in system_rules.biomarkers, which requires a loaded BiomarkerRule (which requires bands). The lab-range-only primitive path is real but structurally inaccessible from system-level scoring without a scoring-engine architecture change.

CONFIRMED. Forbidden file list is comprehensive and explicitly cited in prompt. Deliverables are all under docs/ — no code, no SSOT YAML, no compiled KB, no frontend, no Gemini, no fallback parser paths. CONTENT classification and STOP gates are coherent.

overall_verdict: HARDENED — All five front-matter fields confirmed (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:2-6). All seven authority paths verified to exist in repo. All five prerequisite files confirmed on main. All four P1-6 contamination files confirmed absent from main; failed branch is not merged. CRITICAL ARCHITECTURAL FINDING (from source content cross-reference, not inference): _build_biomarker_rule() at rules.py:131-145 requires all six band sub-keys via direct dict key access — no fallback, no default. BiomarkerRules without bands cannot be constructed; bandless biomarkers listed in a system are silently dropped at rules.py:155. A lab-range-only primitive path exists in calculate_biomarker_score() (rules.py:361-436) but is structurally inaccessible via system orchestration (engine.py:192) for biomarkers not loaded into system_rules.biomarkers. Phase 3 answer is expected to be B: architecture does NOT support lab-range-only thyroid scoring without hardcoded bands. Cursor must reach this conclusion independently from code inspection. If a code change appears required, Cursor must STOP and report — not implement. No SSOT YAML, code, compiled KB, or frontend files may be changed. Deliverables are docs-only. Cursor may proceed under TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH governance.
